context: PR #42 has one must-fix review comment on lib/db/queries.ts:45. A subagent reads the file, proposes a FIX_OLD_STRING/FIX_NEW_STRING, applies it via Edit, and runs `npx tsc --noEmit`, which exits non-zero with a TS2345 error on the changed line.
expected_behavior: The subagent reverts the Edit by swapping old_string/new_string, then confirms `git diff --stat lib/db/queries.ts` shows no changes
Returns VALIDATION_STATUS: failed with VALIDATION_COMMANDS showing `npx tsc --noEmit` and VALIDATION_OUTPUT containing the last ~20 lines of the tsc failure, and downgrades its CONFIDENCE by 1
Step 5 surfaces this fix in the summary table with validation='✗ failed' shown next to the must-fix row, excluded from auto-apply
Step 6 shows the user the proposed diff alongside the failing validation command output ('$ npx tsc --noEmit → 2 (type errors)' and the TS2345 snippet) so they can decide

Auto-apply mode (if selected at Step 4) MUST NOT auto-apply this fix: per the doc, only fixes with confidence>=4 AND validation_status==passed auto-apply; failed/skipped/dirty-cleanup-needed always go to manual review regardless of confidence

If user picks 'Apply fix' anyway, Step 10 reruns the full validation suite and triggers the 'Full validation failed after applying fixes' AskUserQuestion with Revert last fix / Revert all fixes / Commit anyway / Show me the failure options
failure_modes: Subagent fails to revert the Edit after validation and leaves the working tree dirty without reporting VALIDATION_STATUS: dirty-cleanup-needed
Auto-apply mode applies the fix because its CONFIDENCE is still >=4, ignoring the VALIDATION_STATUS: failed rule
Presents the fix as 'verified locally' with a green check despite tsc having failed
